text:
King's College Hospital 

Department of Neurosciences 

Clinic Date: 28 August 2024 

Dr Amelia Carter
Consultant Neurologist   
King's College Hospital  
Department of Neurosciences, King's College Hospital, London SE5 9RS  

Dear Dr Amelia Carter
Consultant Neurologist ,  

Daniel Morris, DOB: 14-03-1988, Hospital No: K372915 NHS No. 9023475612  
18 Brookside Close, London, SE15 2HL

Thank you for your referral and for forwarding the interim notes. I reviewed Daniel Morris in the neurology clinic today. He attended with his seizure diary app, which he has been using consistently since his last appointment, and we were able to corroborate his recent history against the timestamped entries and automated summaries generated by the app.

Seizure pattern has remained largely unchanged since the last clinic visit. He suffers clusters of absence seizures on 2 days each week. Nocturnal tonic seizures continue to occur around once per year. No daytime convulsions have been recorded recently. These data points are supported by his diary entries and caregiver observations, with the weekly summaries showing brief lapses in awareness grouped within the same 24-hour periods, and a single flagged nocturnal event in the past 12 months based on movement and sound alerts recorded overnight.

He reports consistent medication adherence, recorded in the app’s reminder log, without recent dose changes. He denies missed morning doses over the past two months and has set alarms for weekends, which he finds helpful. There is no documented exposure to new precipitants; however, he notes that poor sleep and work-related stress correlate with heavier days in the absence clusters. He does not drive and remains aware of current DVLA guidance. There have been no recent A&E attendances, injuries, or tongue biting, and no family or colleagues have witnessed daytime convulsions.

Neurological examination today was unremarkable. Cognitive screening within clinic limits was stable, and there were no focal deficits. Basic bloods arranged by his GP last month (FBC, U&E, LFTs) were within reference ranges according to the results you provided. He reports intermittent low mood on busier seizure weeks but declines formal psychological input at present; he would reconsider if symptoms persist. He remains engaged with self-management strategies, including sleep hygiene and regular use of the diary app.

Plan:
- Continue current antiseizure regimen with adherence support via the diary app reminders.
- Request app-exported seizure log (CSV/PDF) for upload to the hospital record ahead of the next review.
- Provide written information on managing absence clusters and sleep optimisation, which he accepted.
- Routine follow-up in 6 months, or earlier if there is any change in seizure pattern, emergence of daytime convulsions, injury, or concern from family. He knows how to contact the epilepsy nurse specialist service for interim advice.

Thank you for your ongoing care. Please let me know if there are any concerns or if further information from the diary export would be useful in primary care.

Yours sincerely

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: Identify explicit seizure frequency statements. The letter states: "He suffers clusters of absence seizures on 2 days each week." This indicates clusters occur on two days per week, implying 2 clusters per week (one cluster per day). The number of seizures within each cluster is not specified. Another seizure type occurs about once per year, which is less frequent, so the higher frequency to report is the weekly clusters. Normalize to the allowed cluster format with an unknown per-cluster count.
seizure_frequency_number: 2 cluster per week, multiple per cluster
